questions.question: Will you commit, in writing, to end DOGE's access to sensitive Treasury and IRS systems and to account for the PPP fraud your department is sitting on?
Given the department's recent settlement with the President, how do you keep the number-two job free of political conflicts?
Spare us the crocodile tears on the debt. Show me the math: how does this administration cut the deficit while the tax law adds trillions?

You are moving from Capitol Hill leadership staff into Treasury's chief operating role. Walk us through your ethics agreement, recusals, and how you avoid conflicts with former employers and political patrons.
How will you keep OFAC and the sanctions program fully staffed and enforced under the hiring freeze?
Will you commit to timely, complete written answers to this committee, including the questions Treasury has so far left unanswered?
You helped run the National Economic Council. Who actually pays the current tariffs, and what is your estimate of their cost to American families?
Washington apple growers are getting hit by retaliation. As the operating head, how do you stop tariffs from crushing our exporters?
By one Yale analysis the tariffs cost a typical Georgia family about three times their tax cut. As chief operating officer, what will you do about that?
Who benefits most from the 2025 tax law, and as the department's number two can you defend its split between the wealthiest households and working families?
What management reforms will you pursue to cut waste and speed the President's growth agenda?
What is your view of FSOC and the bank-capital rules, and who watches systemic risk on your watch?
Will you commit to returning federal spending to pre-pandemic levels, and how fast?
You helped run the National Economic Council. Who actually pays the current tariffs, and what is your estimate of their cost to American families?
Will you commit to timely, complete written answers to this committee, including the questions Treasury has so far left unanswered?
Who benefits most from the 2025 tax law, and as the department's number two can you defend its split between the wealthiest households and working families?
Families are squeezed on prices. What will Treasury do on affordability, and do you support the Child Tax Credit?
What management reforms will you pursue to cut waste and speed the President's growth agenda?
The deficit is climbing. What concrete debt-management and spending steps will you take, and do you accept that the tax law adds trillions to the debt?

You would be the number two at a department that just granted the President a favorable tax settlement. Will you release its full legal basis, and do you agree Section 7217 bars any White House role in audits?
Will Treasury support restoring the expanded Child Tax Credit that cut child poverty roughly in half?
Who benefits most from the 2025 tax law, and as the department's number two can you defend its split between the wealthiest households and working families?
As the department's chief operating officer, how do you put Treasury behind a credible, Simpson-Bowles-style path back to fiscal sustainability now that the debt exceeds GDP?
The IRS phone service collapsed after a 40 percent reduction in force. As chief operating officer, will you reverse those cuts?
How should Treasury manage the economic relationship with China, and what is your view of the dollar's reserve status?
How should Treasury manage the economic relationship with China, and what is your view of the dollar's reserve status?
